Title: Unprocessed wheat c-gliadin reduces gluten accumulation associated with the endoplasmic reticulum stress and elevated cell death

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm at 15, 18, 23 and 28 DAP; downregulated in lgp1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve influencing grain filling and dietary carbohydrate properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with inhibited starch biosynthesis in lgp1 endosperm
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling across grain development; TMT proteome support at pathway level
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stages (15–28 DAP) in lgp1 versus WT
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/genome sequencing: PRJNA744310; TMT proteomics: PXD027683
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaAGPS1/2 transcripts were downregulated in lgp1 endosperm. 2. Their reduced expression coincided with lower starch and amylose contents. 3. The change supports impaired AGPase activity/pathway output during grain filling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower starch accumulation and grain weight accompany reduced expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes were not mapped causal genes; they are downstream metabolic genes affected in the mutant.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm at 15, 18, 23 and 28 DAP; downregulated in lgp1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve influencing grain filling and dietary carbohydrate properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression is associated with inhibited starch biosynthesis in lgp1 endosperm
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling across grain development; TMT proteome support at pathway level
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stages (15–28 DAP) in lgp1 versus WT
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/genome sequencing: PRJNA744310; TMT proteomics: PXD027683
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaAGPL1/3 transcripts were downregulated in lgp1 endosperm. 2. Their reduced expression paralleled reduced starch biosynthesis and altered starch granule composition. 3. These genes indicate broad suppression of AGPase-dependent starch synthesis in the mutant.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced starch accumulation and grain filling defects accompany reduced expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream affected genes, not the primary causal mutation.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein lipidation increased in 12 DAP lgp1 seed
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased ATG8-PE indicates enhanced autophagosome formation associated with storage protein loss in lgp1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblot for ATG8-PE; colocalization with mCherry-ATG8e in wheat protoplasts
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 12 DAP developing seed; E-64d inhibitor evidence supports autophagy contribution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lipidated ATG8 accumulated strongly in lgp1. 2. Mutant LGP1 colocalized with ATG8e puncta. 3. Autophagy likely contributes to storage protein degradation in the mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATG8 is used mainly as an autophagy marker here rather than a primary causal nutrient gene.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance increased in 20 and 25 DAP lgp1 endosperm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated BiP marks strong ER stress associated with mutant LGP1 and reduced gluten accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BiP localized to altered ER structures in lgp1. 2. BiP protein levels were markedly increased in mutant endosperm. 3. This supports severe ER stress linked to defective storage protein processing.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Stress marker/chaperone, not the primary causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: γ-gliadin seed storage prolamin with N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Seed storage protein accumulation / gluten deposition pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing endosperm during grain filling; analyzed at 10–28 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing seed/endosperm as a seed storage protein gene
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: γ-gliadin (properly signal-peptide-cleaved in WT); uncleaved γ-gliadin in mutant
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NA; LGP1 is a storage prolamin rather than a catalytic enzyme. Its signal peptide is normally cleaved during secretory processing, enabling proper protein body formation and storage protein deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; protein bodies; extracellular space in mutant
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm protein bodies and protein storage vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutrient reservoir in grain; relevant to dough viscoelasticity and human dietary responses to gluten
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A missense mutation causing signal peptide cleavage failure in LGP1 leads to accumulation of unprocessed γ-gliadin, drastically reduces total gluten proteins, triggers ER stress/autophagosome-like structures/cell death, and indirectly reduces starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; mutant phenotyping; transgenic expression of mutant genomic allele in Fielder; immunoblotting; confocal localization; TEM/immunogold; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native LGP1 promoter (2.4 kb upstream; endogenous promoter) in transgenic complementation/recapitulation assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stages; autophagy inhibitor E-64d treatment increased HMW-glutenin accumulation in lgp1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and genome sequencing: SRA PRJNA744310; TMT proteomics: PXD027683; metabolomics reported in study (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LGP1 was the sole high-confidence gene in the mapped 53.03 kb interval and carries a C-to-T EMS mutation causing Ala19Val at the end of the signal peptide. 2. The mutation prevents proper signal peptide cleavage, causing ER retention/aggregation of mutant γ-gliadin. 3. Mutant LGP1 is sufficient to reproduce the low-gluten phenotype in transgenic wheat. 4. The defect disrupts protein body formation, promotes secretion/degradation of storage proteins, induces ER stress and autophagosome-like structures, elevates cell death, and lowers starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a low-gluten trait donor for specific diets and metabolic engineering; can be introgressed into elite wheat and potentially stacked with gliadin-reduction alleles/editing strategies, but not suitable alone for strict gluten-free products because immunoreactive peptides remain
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced grain size and thousand-grain weight, partially empty pericarp, impaired bread-making quality, reduced starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s title says c-gliadin, but the mapped gene/protein is described throughout the Results as a γ-gliadin. The focal causal mechanism is post-translational processing failure rather than enzymatic catalysis.
